exp_tree1.hypothesis: Gradient-boosted trees capture the non-linear interactions where this dataset's signal lives (linear |r|~0.07); expected to beat the 0.7216 linear floor.

exp_nn1.hypothesis: A 2-layer MLP can learn smooth non-linear interactions on the standardized engineered features; tests whether NN beats trees on this synthetic data.

exp_lin1.hypothesis: L1 sparsity may prune redundant collinear features (cont11/cont12 cluster) from the linear floor.

exp_tree2.hypothesis: Larger min_child_samples regularizes leaf estimates to reduce variance on 240k rows; small expected gain over exp_tree1.

exp_lin2.hypothesis: Blend L1/L2 to balance sparsity and shrinkage; tests the last meaningful linear lever before the class exhausts.

exp_tree3.hypothesis: Column subsampling decorrelates trees for a small additional gain; expected <1% improvement signalling tree-class diminishing returns.

exp_lin3.hypothesis: Heavy L2 shrinkage (alpha=1000) on the exhausted linear class; clearly underperforms the baseline, confirming no linear headroom (stability-confirming variant).
